text: The patient is positioned in the modified lithotomy position. The surgeon excises a disc-shaped section of previously marked skin from the left lower quadrant and enters the abdomen using a muscle-splitting technique.

The surgeon introduces one 12mm and two 5mm trocars through the jet platform. Dissection begins at the right colon, where the ileocolic pedicle is identified and ligated. A medial to lateral dissection is performed, with the duodenum being identified and preserved.
The surgeon completes the mobilization of the ascending colon and performs a clockwise right to left total colectomy, visually detaching the colon from surrounding tissues.

The surgeon takes down the hepatocolic ligament and mobilizes the transverse colon by dividing the mesocolon and the greater omentum. The splenic flexure is taken down, and the left branch of the middle colic artery is ligated. The inferior mesenteric artery remains intact, and the recto-sigmoid junction is stapled.
The surgeon exteriorizes the colon through the ileostomy site. The ileostomy is matured, and the rectal stump is left in the pelvis without implantation.

The surgeon performs a circumferential incision around the stoma site in the left lower quadrant, releasing the loop ileostomy and temporarily sealing it with a trio-viral stick before returning it to the abdomen. Pneumoperitoneum is then established using a single port access device.

The surgeon ligates the inferior mesenteric artery at the level of the superior artery and dissects through the avascular plane posterior to the mesorectum, continuing the dissection down to the levator muscles.

The surgeon continues dissection on both sides upon reaching the levator muscles, followed by anterior mobilization of the rectum. After achieving complete mobilization, a digital rectal exam is performed to confirm the level of dissection.
The surgeon uses a laparoscopic GIA-XVI stapler to apply two staple lines.
The surgeon creates a 15 to 20 centimeter ileal pouch using a stapling device. A leak test is performed on the pouch, which shows no leakage before proceeding with the anastomosis.

The surgeon places a persisting stick with 2-0 Prolene suture and inserts an EEA-28 stapler into the ileal pouch. Under laparoscopic view, intracorporeal anastomosis is created, followed by a pouchoscopy to inspect the anastomosis site.
A loop of bowel proximal to the ileal pouch is exteriorized, and an ileostomy is matured. The intact specimen is displayed.
